### Post by kudos1uk on 2008-03-04
When I startup it says loading (top left) then goes to a black screen before the login screens.

I have an HP NC6400 - ATI Technologies Inc M52 [Mobility Radeon X1300]

Once logged in I use the restricted ATI driver which works fine and I also use Compiz, cube etc which all work well.

I'm guessing something is not correct in xorg

```

# xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#

#

# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg
#Section "Module"
#       Load            "glx"
#EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"

        # Uncomment if you have a wacom tablet
        #       InputDevice     "stylus"        "SendCoreEvents"
        #       InputDevice     "cursor"        "SendCoreEvents"
        #       InputDevice     "eraser"        "SendCoreEvents"
        Identifier     "Default Layout"
        Screen         "Default Screen" 0 0
        InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
        InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
        InputDevice    "Synaptics Touchpad"
EndSection

Section "Files"
EndSection

Section "Module"
EndSection

Section "ServerFlags"
        Option      "AIGLX" "on"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
        Driver      "kbd"
        Option      "CoreKeyboard"
        Option      "XkbRules" "xorg"
        Option      "XkbModel" "pc105"
        Option      "XkbLayout" "gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
        Driver      "mouse"
        Option      "CorePointer"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
        Option      "Protocol" "ImPS/2"
        Option      "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
        Option      "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "Synaptics Touchpad"
        Driver      "synaptics"
        Option      "SendCoreEvents" "true"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/psaux"
        Option      "Protocol" "auto-dev"
        Option      "HorizEdgeScroll" "0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "stylus"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option      "Type" "stylus"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "eraser"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option      "Type" "eraser"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
        Identifier  "cursor"
        Driver      "wacom"
        Option      "Device" "/dev/input/wacom"
        Option      "Type" "cursor"
        Option      "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier   "Generic Monitor"
        HorizSync    30.0 - 70.0
        VertRefresh  50.0 - 160.0
        Option      "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier  "Generic Video Card"
        Driver      "fglrx"
        Option      "VideoOverlay" "on"
        Option      "OpenGLOverlay" "off"
        BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier "Default Screen"
        Device     "Generic Video Card"
        Monitor    "Generic Monitor"
        DefaultDepth     24
EndSection

Section "Extensions"
        Option      "Composite" "disable"
EndSection

```

Any help or advice would be really welcome.

### Post by plucky on 2008-03-04
kudos1uk,

Try this
```
gksudo gedit /etc/usplash.conf
```
Set the **xres=1024** and the **yres=768** or the default for your monitor.

save and reboot.

If that doesn't work,then try ```
gksudo gedit /boot/grub/menu.lst
``` and add **vga=791** to the kernel line in menu.lst e.g
 > kernel          /boot/vmlinuz-2.6.22-14-generic root=UUID=459b742d-38d5-4182-8c07-d4e45df4c923 ro quiet splash vga=791

Good luck

---

### Post by Lowcountry on 2008-03-04
I use startupmanager.
I'm a gui guy.

### Post by kellsens on 2008-03-04
Hi, Kudos1K !

   After you edit the /etc/usplash.conf file, like plucky said, try this :

   ```
sudo update-usplash-theme usplash-theme-ubuntu
```

---

```
Set the **xres=1024** and the **yres=768** or the default for your monitor.

save and reboot.

If that doesn't work,then try ```
gksudo gedit /boot/grub/menu.lst
``` and add **vga=791** to the kernel line in menu.lst e.g
 

Good luck

Many thanks plucky The first part of your advice gave me a shut-down splash 

It was set to **xres=1224** and the **yres=1080** 
I changed it so match my setup: **xres=1224** and the **yres=800**

and the second part gave me a startup splash. 


Many thanks for your help on this.
